content.value: I designed a campus commuting app that helps students build lasting connections by matching them with others walking similar routes between classes.
Based on user interviews, the design emphasizes consistent engagement outside the classroom as the best way to develop rapport.
By encouraging recurring meetups during commutes, the app makes it easy to meet people who also want to expand their social circles.

To add a playful and personal touch, the app features a customizable sticker board: users earn stickers, display them as conversation starters, and exchange them after meetups—emulating the way laptop stickers carry stories and memories in real life, but in a digital form.

content.value: I designed a digital intake system for food banks to replace slow paper-based forms.
The prototype streamlines client onboarding, collects dietary and resource needs often missed in the manual process, and saves valuable volunteer time.

Built through storyboarding, iterative design, and high-fidelity prototyping in Figma, the project aims to improve accessibility, service quality, and scalability for food banks and their communities.

takes all the dietary restrictions and allergies of the household members. Clean and simple with a table to collect dietary restrictions, gauge severity, a notes section, and culinary preferences in a drop down
